capabilities.title: Skill paths glob activation
capabilities.description: A skill auto-activates when an edited file matches a declared glob. Authored canonically as x-claude.paths and projected to Claude-native paths: frontmatter; replaces manual language-overlay routing.

capabilities.title: Skill agent auto-dispatch
capabilities.description: A skill declares a paired subagent and is auto-dispatched into it (e.g. code-review skill -> code-reviewer agent). Authored as x-claude.agent and projected to Claude-native agent: frontmatter.

capabilities.title: Skill disallowed-tools frontmatter
capabilities.description: Tools removed from the model's pool while a skill is active (per-turn, clears on next user message). Authored as x-claude.disallowed-tools; read-only skills (code-review, security-auditing, etc.) disallow Edit/Write/NotebookEdit.

capabilities.title: Agent tools allowlist permissions
capabilities.description: An agent declares a tools: allowlist (or disallowedTools: denylist) restricting which tools the subagent may call. Part of the parallel permissions deliverable.

capabilities.title: Plugin userConfig install prompts
capabilities.description: Plugin manifest declares user-configurable values prompted at enable time (e.g. preferredLanguageOverlay, telemetryOptIn). Values flow into ${USER_CONFIG_*} substitutions exposed to skills.

capabilities.title: Plugin defaultEnabled flag
capabilities.description: plugin.json/marketplace field controlling whether the plugin installs in an enabled state. Older Claude Code ignores it and enables on install (v2.1.154+ floor).

capabilities.title: Background monitors
capabilities.description: Plugin-declared shell commands whose stdout lines arrive as session notifications, wired via Claude plugin.json experimental.monitors. OPT-IN since the default plugin.json declares no monitors (a fresh install fires none); Aegis ships cost-watcher.sh for users who opt in. The context-window monitor was retired — a monitor gets no session JSON, so accurate context usage comes from the statusline context segment instead. Requires Claude Code v2.1.105+; interactive CLI only.

capabilities.title: Plugin dependencies and semver constraints
capabilities.description: plugin.json declares dependencies with version constraints so a plugin holds an upstream at a tested range. Aegis emits an empty dependencies: [] skeleton for now; the aegis-core/aegis-languages split is deferred to a later release.

capabilities.title: ZIP / .skill distribution
capabilities.description: Additive distribution channel: dist/aegis.skill is a reproducible ZIP of the projected Claude tree loadable via claude --plugin-url. Coexists with the git-spec install paths; built on demand by scripts/build-dist-zip.mjs.

capabilities.title: Statusline presets
capabilities.description: 8 statusline presets rendered by bulletproof zero-dependency Node ESM, wired into settings.json via the /aegis:statusline command. pr segment reads native statusline JSON with no gh shell-out.

capabilities.title: Templates surface
capabilities.description: Canonical templates/ tree (markdown/json/html) referenced by skills via the ${TEMPLATE:<family>} build-time directive, plus the v0.1.1 runtime index→Read procedure (rules/user-choice-discipline.md step 5) that lets an agent resolve a user-chosen format (incl. HTML) at runtime. Claude closes both mechanisms end-to-end via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}. Codex bundles the html/json templates + template-index.json (fixing the prior :json bundled-pointer gap) but has no equivalent base-path token for the runtime read, so it stays partial. OpenCode has no plugin-root token at all — the ${TEMPLATE} directive renders literally and the runtime read has no resolvable base path — also partial. Cursor/Zed fall back to a literal file-path reference (deferred).

capabilities.title: Model intent tiers
capabilities.description: An agent declares a provider-neutral capability-intent tier (deep|balanced|fast|inherit) in manifest/permissions.json; manifest/models.json resolves it to a Claude-native model ID at projection time. Retired Anthropic-family names (opus/sonnet/haiku) still resolve through aliasOf. Unknown alias is a hard fail. Claude is the ONLY host where a tier resolves to anything: Aegis emits no model: field on OpenCode or Codex, so those hosts own model selection entirely.

capabilities.title: Provider-tagged prose forking
capabilities.description: Inline <claude>...</claude> / <opencode>...</opencode> blocks in skill bodies kept for the matching host and stripped elsewhere at projection time. Soft cap of 3 blocks per file (validator warns above).

capabilities.title: SessionStart hook
capabilities.description: Lifecycle hook fired on session startup/clear/compact, used to deliver the Aegis bootstrap pointer (additionalContext) since no plugin-root CLAUDE.md is loaded.

capabilities.title: PreCompact/PostCompact hooks
capabilities.description: Portable hook intent pair: captures decision/test anchors before compaction and restores them after. Claude PreCompact/PostCompact command hooks (full projection). On OpenCode only pre-compact has a home — it binds the flat dotted key experimental.session.compacting, which fires once before compaction; post-compact is Claude-only because OpenCode exposes no post-compaction context-injection hook. The registered pre-compact handler body is a deliberate no-op placeholder (no durable anchor store).

capabilities.title: InstructionsLoaded hook
capabilities.description: Portable hook intent: Claude InstructionsLoaded command hook reporting loaded-rule count and silent drops, consumed by the using-aegis skill's health-check fragment. No counterpart on other hosts.

capabilities.title: MCP servers
capabilities.description: Model Context Protocol server entries. Claude/OpenCode/Cursor/Zed load native MCP config; Codex ships an empty mcp.json stub with real servers deferred to a later release.

capabilities.title: Native subagent memory
capabilities.description: x-claude.memory: <scope> (user|project|local) projects to native memory: in the generated Claude agent frontmatter. Claude auto-injects the first 200 lines/25 KB of MEMORY.md into the subagent system prompt and auto-enables Read/Write/Edit (cc-docs sub-agents.md §453-490). Non-Claude hosts have no native equivalent; fallback is a plain .aegis-memory/MEMORY.md read by the host-neutral recall fragment of using-aegis. Decay/cross-session ranking deferred. See rules/memory-discipline.md for the observation taxonomy and skills/core/using-aegis/abilities/recall.md for the 3-layer retrieval workflow.
